implementation_steps.exit_gate: GGUF reader lists classifier head tensors and metadata without requiring sidecar files.
implementation_steps.work: Persist score-head tensors and label metadata in GGUF instead of an NPZ sidecar. Record num_labels, label order, pooling policy, and problem type.

implementation_steps.exit_gate: Single-sample logits match PyTorch/sidecar logits within a strict relative RMS threshold.
implementation_steps.step: 2. Add native Qwen sequence-classification graph path
implementation_steps.work: Reuse the audited `bitnet-qwen` decoder semantics, then pool the last non-padding token and apply the dense score head in the runtime.

implementation_steps.exit_gate: Full MNLI validation runs from one native GGUF artifact and reports accuracy, RSS, and examples/sec.
implementation_steps.step: 3. Implement a CPU GLUE classifier evaluator
implementation_steps.work: Replace the Python sidecar loop with native GGUF classifier inference and preserve the same prompt/tokenization contract used by PyTorch validation.

implementation_steps.exit_gate: Batch-size 1 and production batch size have identical predictions on a fixed validation subset.
implementation_steps.work: Native batching must preserve predictions relative to batch size 1. Existing separator batching changed `3/64` sidecar predictions, so it is not a safe semantic reference.

implementation_steps.exit_gate: same_artifact_quality_cpu_ready=true and coverage gate passes with native classifier rows.
implementation_steps.work: Only after native logits, full-split accuracy, RSS, and throughput are present should the sequence-classification product status move from prototype to deployed artifact.

verdict: Do not call the sequence-classification artifact deployed yet. The sidecar proves the path is plausible, but native GGUF score-head metadata, runtime pooling/head execution, full validation accuracy, RSS, and batching parity are still required.
